{config_emoji} Personal Configuration
    *View or adjust personal settings with the {cmd_my} command.*
    {cmd_my_timezone}: Timezone used to display your stats and set reminders.
    {cmd_my_language}: Your preferred language for commands and interactions.


    {stats_emoji} Statistics
    *Study in voice channels to earn activity ranks and compete on the leaderboard!*
    {cmd_me}: View your personal study profile and set your profile tags.
    {cmd_stats}: View study statistics for the current and past weeks or months.
    {cmd_ranks}: See the list of activity ranks.
    {cmd_leaderboard}: Compete with other members on the server leaderboards.


    {coin} Economy
    *Earn coins through studying, then spend them on some well deserved rewards!*
    {cmd_send}: Send your {coin} to another member.
    {cmd_shop}: Purchase server roles with your {coin}.
    {cmd_room}: Rent a private voice channel for you and your friends.


    {utility_emoji} Utilities
    *Some other utilities to help you stay productive while studying!*
    {cmd_reminders}: Ask me to remind you about that important task!
    {cmd_tasklist}: Create tasks and feel the satisfaction of checking them off.
    {cmd_timers}: Stay productive using the classic *pomodoro technique*!
    {cmd_schedule}: Schedule a shared study session and keep yourself accountable!

Use {cmd_dashboard} to see an overview of the server configuration,     and quickly jump to the feature configuration panels to modify settings.

    Most settings may also be directly set through the `/config` and `/admin config` commands,     depending on whether the settings require moderator (manage server) or admin level permissions, respectively.

    Other relevant commands for guild configuration below:
    `/editshop`: Add/Edit/Remove colour roles from the {coin} shop.
    `/ranks`: Add/Edit/Refresh/Remove activity ranks.
    `/pomodoro`: Add/Edit/Remove Pomodoro timers in voice channels.
    `/rolemenus`: Allow members to equip roles from customisable messages.
    `/economy balance`: Display and modify LionCoin balance for members and roles.

Thank you for inviting me to your community!

strings.default: This interface will time out {timestamp}. Press 'Continue' below to keep editing.
Role given when members are blacklisted from video channels.
Not Set. (Members will not be automatically blacklisted.)

This role will be automatically given after a member has failed to keep their video enabled in a video channel (see above).
Members who have this role will not be able to join configured video channels. The role permissions may be freely configured by server admins to place further restrictions on the offender.
The role may also be manually assigned, to the same effect.
If this role is not set, no video blacklist will occur, and members will only be kicked from the channel and warned.
Members who fail to keep their video on will be given {role}
Members will no longer be automatically blacklisted from video channels.
Comma separated channel ids or names.
List of voice channels and categories in which to enforce video.

Member will be required to turn on their video in these channels.
If they do not enable their video with `15` seconds of joining, they will be asked to enable it through a notification in direct messages or the `alert_channel`. If they still have not enabled it after the `video_grace_period` has passed, they will be kicked from the channel. Further, after the first offence (which is considered a warning), they will be given the `video_blacklist` role, if configured, which will stop them from joining video channels.
As usual, if a category is configured, this will apply to all voice channels under the category.
Members will be asked to turn on their video in the following channels: {channels}
Members will not be asked to turn on their video in any channels.
Sequence of durations for automatic video blacklists.

When `video_blacklist` is set and members fail to turn on their video within the configured `video_grace_period`, they will be automatically blacklisted (i.e. given the `video_blacklist` role).
This setting describes *how long* the member will be blacklisted for, for each offence.
E.g. if this is set to `1d, 7d, 30d`, then on the first offence the member will be blacklisted for 1 day, on the second for 7 days, and on the third for 30 days. A subsequent offence will result in an infinite blacklist.
Members will be automatically blacklisted for: {durations}
Video blacklists are now always permanent.
List of exempt role names or ids.
List of roles which are exempt from video channels.
Members who have **any** of these roles will not be required to enable their video in the `video_channels`. This also overrides the `video_blacklist` role.
The following roles will now be exempt from video channels: {roles}
No members will be exempt from video channel requirements.
How many seconds to wait for a member to enable video.
How long to wait (in seconds) before kicking/blacklist members who don't enable their video.

The length of time a member has to enable their video after joining a video channel. After this time, if they have not enabled their video, they will be kicked from the channel and potentially blacklisted from video channels.
Members will now have **{duration}** to enable their video.
